mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals strong consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings upward and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. This mechanism is always active as retail sales are a coincident indicator of demand.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year rolling window the Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth with 20-day forward returns is below 0.03, or if recessionary periods show negative correlation, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 4.5%, labor market slack becomes evident, reducing wage growth expectations and consumer confidence, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 4.5% but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated interest rates (FEDFUNDS above 4%) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE increasing month-over-month) create a stagflationary signal that compresses equity multiples via higher discount rates and lower earnings expectations, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences, or if the mean return is not significantly below zero (p>0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime defined by CPIAUCSL above 310, persistent price pressures force the Fed to maintain or raise rates, which depresses equity valuations via higher discount rates. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL exceeds 310 but S&P 500 20-day returns average positive or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Solid industrial production growth, measured by INDPRO month-over-month increases, indicates supply-side strength and robust manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the month-over-month growth rate of INDPRO has a Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST below 1,300,000) signals weakening residential investment and consumer sentiment, which typically precedes economic slowdown and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If HOUST falls below 1300 but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or if the average return is not significantly negative (p>0.05), the hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) show sequential monthly gains, the economy is in a demand-led expansion that boosts earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction amplifies the signal beyond each series individually.
failure_condition: If the joint positive signal (both series up month-over-month) does not yield a significantly positive mean 20-day return (t-test p>0.05) or the proportion of positive returns is below 60%, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle when the Fed raises rates (FEDFUNDS rising above its 12-month moving average), further increases compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If, when FEDFUNDS exceeds its 12-month moving average, the 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) or the proportion of negative returns is below 55% over any 2-year window, the hypothesis fails.
